name: Soy yogurt (food medium)

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380: total sugars 8.04 g)
components.evidence: Dominant fermentable sugar; total USDA sugars 8.04 g/100g is overwhelmingly sucrose (commercial soy yogurt is sweetened with cane sucrose; native soy sucrose is largely fermented). Sucrose is the principal sugar consumed by LAB in soy fermentation (AEM 10.1128/aem.01936-23).

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: Fermented soy beverage residual sugars, PMC10485619 (galactose 18.8-29.8 mg%)
components.evidence: Residual monosaccharide released by microbial hydrolysis of raffinose-family oligosaccharides during fermentation.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Fermented soy beverage residual sugars, PMC10485619 (glucose 9.1-31.3 mg%)
components.evidence: Residual monosaccharide after fermentation; readily fermentable.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: Fermented soy beverage residual sugars, PMC10485619 (fructose 4.7-12.9 mg%)
components.evidence: Minor residual monosaccharide.

components.name: stachyose
components.source: Fermented soy beverage residual sugars, PMC10485619 (stachyose 9.9-21.4 mg%)
components.evidence: Soy raffinose-family oligosaccharide; partially fermented (strain-dependent), remainder residual.

components.name: raffinose
components.source: Fermented soy beverage residual sugars, PMC10485619 (raffinose 1.3-4.3 mg%)
components.evidence: Soy oligosaccharide; largely consumed by RFO-utilizing starters.

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: Titratable acidity of soy yogurt, PMC6595013 (0.56-1.17% as lactic acid)
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid and defining metabolite of the fermented product; primary fermentation end-product from sucrose. Midpoint of measured titratable acidity range.

components.name: acetic acid
components.source: Lactic/acetic ratio in soy yogurt 15.5-22.3, PMC6902289 (estimate from lactic acid = 0.8 g)
components.evidence: Secondary fermentation acid; heterofermentative co-product, present at ~5-6% of lactic acid.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: HPLC organic-acid panels of fermented soymilk detect citrate (Frontiers fnut.2023.1198456; PMC6902289)
components.evidence: Native soybean organic acid, present but not quantified for this product; partially consumed during fermentation.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: HPLC organic-acid standard panels for fermented soy include malate (Frontiers fnut.2023.1198456)
components.evidence: Native soy organic acid, present but unquantified for this product.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: HPLC organic-acid panels of fermented soymilk detect succinate (Frontiers fnut.2023.1198456; PMC6902289)
components.evidence: Minor fermentation/TCA-derived acid, present but unquantified.

components.name: total protein
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Soy protein; partially hydrolysed to peptides and free amino acids by fermentation. Amino-acid rows below are the soy-protein profile scaled to this protein content.

components.name: L-glutamic acid
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein (factor 1.477)
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid in soy protein.

components.name: L-aspartic acid
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Second most abundant soy amino acid.

components.name: L-arginine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein

components.name: L-leucine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Essential amino acid.

components.name: L-proline
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein

components.name: L-serine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein

components.name: L-lysine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Essential amino acid; soy is lysine-rich.

components.name: L-valine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Essential amino acid.

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Essential amino acid.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Essential amino acid.

components.name: L-threonine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Essential amino acid.

components.name: glycine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein

components.name: L-alanine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein

components.name: L-histidine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Essential amino acid.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Essential amino acid.

components.name: L-methionine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile scaled to 4.83 g protein
components.evidence: Essential; soy's limiting sulfur amino acid.

components.name: L-cystine
components.source: USDA FDC Soymilk unfortified AA profile (reported 0/not measured)
components.evidence: Present in soy protein (~1.3% of protein) but not quantified in the source; sulfur amino acid.

components.name: ammonium
components.source: Product of proteolysis/deamidation during fermentation (FooDB FOOD00767)
components.evidence: Free ammonium nitrogen source, plausible in fermented soy; unquantified.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.source: USDA total PUFA 1.45 g apportioned by soybean-oil FA profile (~51% linoleic)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid of soybean oil; major polyunsaturated fatty acid.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.source: USDA total MUFA 0.55 g (soybean-oil profile, oleic ~23% of oil)
components.evidence: Principal monounsaturated fatty acid.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.source: USDA total PUFA 1.45 g apportioned by soybean-oil FA profile (~7-8% ALA)
components.evidence: Omega-3 fatty acid of soybean oil.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.source: USDA total saturated fat 0.39 g apportioned by soybean-oil FA profile (~11%)
components.evidence: Main saturated fatty acid.

components.name: stearic acid (18:0)
components.source: USDA total saturated fat 0.39 g apportioned by soybean-oil FA profile (~4%)
components.evidence: Minor saturated fatty acid.

components.name: choline
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Naturally abundant in soy.

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)

components.name: thiamine (B1)
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Elevated by microbial synthesis during fermentation.

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)

components.name: pantothenic acid (B5)
components.source: Estimate from USDA FDC Soymilk (0.37 mg/100g), scaled; not in USDA soy-yogurt panel
components.evidence: Present in soy base; approximate value pending product-specific measurement.

components.name: pyridoxine (B6)
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)

components.name: folate (B9)
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Often increased by LAB folate synthesis in soy fermentation.

components.name: vitamin B12
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Fortification-derived (soy contains no native B12).

components.name: retinol / vitamin A
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Fortification-derived.

components.name: alpha-tocopherol (vitamin E)
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: From soybean oil.

components.name: vitamin D
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Fortification-derived.

components.name: phytomenadione (vitamin K1)
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: From soybean oil.

components.name: potassium
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Major cation.

components.name: calcium
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Partly fortification-derived in commercial soy yogurt.

components.name: phosphorus (phosphate)
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Phosphate source.

components.name: sodium
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)

components.name: magnesium
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)

components.name: iron
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)
components.evidence: Consolidates redundant Fe2+/Fe3+ FooDB entries.

components.name: zinc
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)

components.name: copper
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)

components.name: manganese
components.source: Estimate from USDA FDC Soymilk (0.223 mg/100g); not in USDA soy-yogurt panel
components.evidence: Present in soy base; approximate.

components.name: selenium
components.source: USDA FDC 'Yogurt, soy' (nutritionvalue.org 41420380)

components.name: chloride
components.source: FooDB FOOD00767
components.evidence: Counter-ion, present but unquantified in USDA panel.

components.name: sulfate
components.source: FooDB FOOD00767 / soy sulfur amino acids
components.evidence: Sulfur source, present but unquantified.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a defined growth nutrient of soy yogurt; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: free cobalt ion is FooDB trace/predicted noise; biological cobalt in this food is confined to fortified B12, already listed

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: molybdenum present only at negligible/unquantified trace level; FooDB predicted, not a meaningful medium component

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: compartment-tagged model artifact and duplicate of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: redundant duplicate iron species; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry

missing_added: lactic acid (dominant organic acid / defining fermentation metabolite - was absent)
citric acid, malic acid, succinic acid (native soy organic acids, present-but-unquantified)
specific sugars split out: glucose, fructose, galactose, stachyose, raffinose (soy oligosaccharides) beyond sucrose
full free/protein amino acid profile (18 amino acids)
major fatty acids: linoleic, oleic, alpha-linolenic, palmitic, stearic
pantothenic acid (B5), niacin (B3), riboflavin, vitamin B12, vitamin E, vitamin K, vitamin C amounts
quantified minerals incl. potassium, phosphorus, magnesium, sodium, zinc, copper, selenium, manganese
keep_uncovered: genistin, daidzin, glycitin and their 6''-malonyl/6''-acetyl conjugates (soy isoflavone glycosides)
sources: USDA FoodData Central 'Yogurt, soy' (full nutrient panel; via nutritionvalue.org record 41420380)
USDA FoodData Central 'Soymilk, unfortified' (amino-acid profile, B5, Mn; via nutritionvalue.org)
Titratable acidity of soy yogurt 0.56-1.17% as lactic acid, PMC6595013
Lactic/acetic ratio in soy yogurt (15.5-22.3), PMC6902289
Residual sugars of fermented soy beverage (glucose/galactose/fructose/stachyose/raffinose/sucrose mg%), PMC10485619
Initial soy-juice sugar profile and LAB sucrose metabolism, AEM 10.1128/aem.01936-23
Organic-acid and amino-acid changes on soy fermentation, Frontiers in Nutrition 10.3389/fnut.2023.1198456
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe on this medium are (1) residual/added SUCROSE (~7.9 g/100g, largely added cane sugar in the sweetened USDA reference product) and (2) LACTIC ACID (~0.8 g/100g), the defining fermentation metabolite that was entirely missing from the FooDB profile; secondary substrates are free amino acids/peptides from partially proteolysed soy protein and the residual soy oligosaccharides stachyose/raffinose. Unlike dairy yogurt this food contains NO lactose. Sugar total anchors to the sweetened USDA 'Yogurt, soy' entry (8.04 g total sugars); an unsweetened plain soy yogurt would carry far less sucrose and correspondingly higher relative oligosaccharide share.
